operating_state: Total revenues increased 62% to $61.9 billion driven by Google Search growth, YouTube advertising expansion, and Google Cloud acceleration. Google Services operating income increased $12.8 billion year-over-year while Google Cloud operating loss decreased $835 million as revenue growth outpaced expense increases. Regional performance showed balanced growth with broad strength across search, YouTube, and network properties.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased 41% to $26.2 billion with TAC rising 63% though TAC rate decreased to 21.7% from revenue mix shift toward Google properties. Extended useful life estimates for servers and network equipment reduced depreciation expense by $721 million. General and administrative expenses increased primarily from $796 million in legal matter charges, partially offset by reduced credit loss allowances.
material_events: Completed Fitbit acquisition for $2.1 billion in January 2021 and repurchased $12.8 billion of capital stock during the quarter under new $50 billion authorization. Waymo completed $2.5 billion investment round with majority funding from Alphabet and external investors. Supreme Court ruled in Google's favor in Oracle Java API case, concluding the litigation. Company established new $10 billion revolving credit facilities with sustainability-linked interest rates.
macro_exposures: Continued benefit from elevated consumer online activity and broad-based advertiser spending recovery following COVID-19 impacts. Foreign currency exchange rates favorably affected international revenues as U.S. dollar weakened relative to major currencies. Investment portfolio volatility contributed $5.8 billion increase in other income from unrealized gains on equity securities.
forward_outlook: Management expects revenue growth rate effects from server useful life depreciation change to decline through remainder of 2021. Continued investment focus on technical infrastructure, data centers, and growth initiatives across ads, Google Cloud, Search, and YouTube. Multi-year ERP system implementation continues with subledger completion achieved in Q2 2021.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple ongoing antitrust investigations including DOJ lawsuit regarding Search and Search advertising, state attorneys general complaints regarding advertising technology, and European Commission formal investigation into advertising technology practices opened June 2021. European Commission fines totaling $9.5 billion remain under appeal with bank guarantees provided. Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $4.6 billion with long-term transition tax payable continuing through 2025.
